title: Care Hospital Appointment Booking System - Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Implement sticky glassmorphism header with navigation and mobile responsive menu
acceptanceCriteria: Header remains fixed at the top while scrolling
Glassmorphism effect (frosted glass background) is visible on the navbar
Logo with hospital icon and 'CARE HOSPITAL' text appears on the left
All 6 nav links render and scroll/navigate to their respective sections
Login and Register buttons are visible on desktop
Emergency button is highlighted in red
On mobile, a hamburger icon appears and opens an animated slide-in drawer menu

file_operations.description: Create Header component with sticky positioning, glassmorphism styling (backdrop-blur-md, bg-white/80), CARE HOSPITAL logo using the generated image at frontend/public/assets/generated/care-hospital-logo.dim_128x128.png, navigation links (Home, About, Doctors, Departments, Book Appointment, Contact), Login/Register buttons, Emergency button with red highlight, and mobile hamburger menu with animated slide-in drawer using Framer Motion
Import and render Header component at the top of the application layout, ensure it appears above all other sections

requirements.summary: Build animated hero section with headline, CTAs, and doctor illustration
acceptanceCriteria: Headline and subtext render prominently on the left
Both CTA buttons are present and functional (scroll to respective sections)
A doctor/hospital illustration image is displayed on the right side
Entrance animations play on page load using Framer Motion
Section is fully responsive (stacks vertically on mobile)

file_operations.description: Create HeroSection component with headline 'Your Health, Our Priority', subtext 'Book appointments with top doctors instantly.', two CTA buttons ('Book Appointment' primary blue and 'View Doctors' secondary), doctor illustration using the generated image at frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-doctor.dim_600x500.png on the right, Framer Motion entrance animations (fade-in and slide-up), responsive flex layout that stacks vertically on mobile
Import and render HeroSection component below the Header in the main layout

requirements.summary: Create Doctors section with responsive grid of doctor cards
acceptanceCriteria: At least 6 doctor cards are displayed in a 3-column grid on desktop
Each card shows photo, name, specialization, experience, star rating, and Book Now button
Grid collapses to 2 columns on tablet and 1 column on mobile
Cards have soft shadows and rounded corners
Hover effects are visible on each card

file_operations.description: Create DoctorsSection component with responsive grid (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3), at least 6 doctor cards with sample data including photo placeholders, name, specialization (varied: Cardiology, Neurology, Orthopedics, Pediatrics, Dermatology, General Medicine), years of experience, star rating display, Book Now button, soft shadows (shadow-md), rounded corners (rounded-lg), and hover effects (hover:shadow-xl transition)

Create reusable DoctorCard component accepting doctor props (photo, name, specialization, experience, rating) and rendering card with consistent medical theme styling, hover scale effect, and Book Now button that navigates to booking section
Import and render DoctorsSection component in the main layout below HeroSection, add id='doctors' for scroll navigation

requirements.summary: Build Departments section with animated hover cards
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 departments are displayed as cards with icons
Hover animation is applied to each card
Cards are arranged in a responsive grid
Each card displays the department name and a relevant medical icon

file_operations.description: Create DepartmentsSection component with responsive grid displaying 6 department cards: Cardiology, Neurology, Orthopedics, Pediatrics, Dermatology, General Medicine. Each card includes relevant Lucide React icons (Heart, Brain, Bone, Baby, Sparkles, Stethoscope), department name, hover scale animation using Framer Motion, soft shadows, rounded corners, and blue-to-green gradient accents
Import and render DepartmentsSection component in the main layout below DoctorsSection, add id='departments' for scroll navigation

requirements.summary: Implement appointment booking form with validation and backend integration
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 form fields are present and functional
Client-side validation highlights missing or invalid fields
A success confirmation message is shown after valid submission
Doctor and Department dropdowns are populated with available options
Date picker prevents selection of past dates
Submitted appointments are persisted in the backend
Form resets after successful submission

file_operations.description: Create BookingSection component with appointment form containing fields: Patient Name (text input), Email (email input with validation), Phone (tel input), Select Doctor (dropdown populated from DoctorsSection data), Select Department (dropdown with 6 departments), Date Picker (prevents past dates using min attribute), Time Slot selector (dropdown with common time slots). Implement client-side validation using React Hook Form or native validation, display error messages for invalid fields, show success message on submission, call backend submitAppointment function via useActor hook, reset form after successful submission. Use the authorization component to require user login before booking.

Create custom hook useSubmitAppointment using React Query's useMutation to call backend submitAppointment function. Handle loading states, success callback to show confirmation message, error handling for failed submissions. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Import and render BookingSection component in the main layout below DepartmentsSection, add id='book-appointment' for scroll navigation. Ensure the section is only accessible to authenticated users by wrapping with authorization check.

requirements.summary: Add Features section with animated feature cards
acceptanceCriteria: All 4 feature cards are displayed in a responsive grid
Each card has an icon, title, and short description
Scroll-triggered entrance animations are applied via Framer Motion
Cards have consistent styling with soft shadows and rounded corners

file_operations.description: Create FeaturesSection component with responsive grid displaying 4 feature cards: '24/7 Emergency Support' (Clock icon), 'Experienced Doctors' (UserCheck icon), 'Online Reports' (FileText icon), 'Easy Appointment Booking' (Calendar icon). Each card includes Lucide React icon, title, brief description, scroll-triggered entrance animations using Framer Motion's whileInView, soft shadows (shadow-lg), rounded corners (rounded-xl), and consistent medical theme styling with blue/green accents
Import and render FeaturesSection component in the main layout below BookingSection

requirements.summary: Build Contact section with map, contact details, and contact form
acceptanceCriteria: A Google Maps iframe is embedded showing a placeholder hospital location
Contact details (address, phone, email, working hours) are displayed
A contact form with Name, Email, and Message fields is present
Section is responsive and displays correctly on all screen sizes

file_operations.description: Create ContactSection component with two-column layout: left side contains Google Maps iframe embed (using static iframe with sample hospital location coordinates), right side displays contact details (address with MapPin icon, phone with Phone icon, email with Mail icon, working hours with Clock icon) and quick contact form with Name, Email, Message textarea fields. Form includes client-side validation and submit handler. Responsive layout stacks vertically on mobile. Use medical theme colors and styling.
Import and render ContactSection component in the main layout below FeaturesSection, add id='contact' for scroll navigation

requirements.summary: Create professional multi-column footer with links and social media icons
acceptanceCriteria: Footer has a dark blue background with white text
All 4 columns are present with correct content
Social media icons are visible and styled
Bottom bar shows copyright text and policy links
Footer collapses gracefully on mobile (stacked columns)

file_operations.description: Create Footer component with dark blue background (bg-blue-900), white text (text-white), 4-column grid layout: (1) About Hospital with links (About Us, Our Services, Careers, Blog), (2) Quick Links (Book Appointment, Find Doctor, Departments, Emergency), (3) Contact Info (address, phone, email, working hours with icons), (4) Social Media icons (Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn using Lucide React icons). Include bottom bar with '© 2026 CARE HOSPITAL. All Rights Reserved.' and Privacy Policy / Terms & Conditions links. Responsive grid collapses to stacked columns on mobile.
Import and render Footer component at the bottom of the application layout after all sections

requirements.summary: Apply consistent medical design theme across the application
acceptanceCriteria: White, blue, and light green gradient palette is used consistently throughout
Poppins or Inter font is applied globally
All cards and containers use soft shadows and rounded corners
Hover effects are present on all interactive elements
No horizontal overflow issues on any screen size
Layout is properly spaced and readable on mobile, tablet, and desktop

file_operations.description: Import Poppins or Inter font from Google Fonts, apply font-family globally to body, define CSS custom properties for medical theme colors (white, blue shades, light green gradient), ensure base styles prevent horizontal overflow (overflow-x: hidden), set consistent spacing and typography scales

Extend Tailwind theme with medical color palette (blue-primary, blue-secondary, green-accent, white, light grays), set Poppins or Inter as default font family, configure custom shadows for soft effects, add rounded corner utilities, configure hover transition defaults, ensure responsive breakpoints are properly configured

requirements.summary: Integrate authentication UI with Internet Identity for booking access
acceptanceCriteria: Login and Register buttons trigger Internet Identity authentication flow
User profile setup modal appears on first login to collect user name and email
Authenticated users can access booking section
User profile name is displayed instead of principal ID
Logout clears cached data and returns to guest view

file_operations.description: Create LoginButton component using the authorization component's useInternetIdentity hook to handle login/logout. Display 'Login' when not authenticated, 'Logout' when authenticated, handle loading states during authentication. Use the authorization component to manage authentication state. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create ProfileSetupModal component that appears when user logs in for the first time (getCallerUserProfile returns null). Modal contains form to collect user name and email, calls saveCallerUserProfile to persist profile. Implement proper loading state handling to prevent modal flash as described in authorization component usage. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create custom hook useGetCallerUserProfile using React Query to fetch current user's profile via getCallerUserProfile backend function. Implement proper loading state management to prevent modal flash, handle actor dependency as specified in authorization component usage. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create custom hook useSaveCallerUserProfile using React Query's useMutation to save user profile via saveCallerUserProfile backend function. Handle success callback to invalidate and refetch profile query. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Replace placeholder Login and Register buttons with LoginButton component. Display user profile name when authenticated instead of Login button. Add ProfileSetupModal component that conditionally renders based on authentication and profile status.

Add authentication guard to BookingSection: show message prompting user to login if not authenticated, only render booking form for authenticated users. Use useInternetIdentity hook to check authentication status.
Wrap application with ProfileSetupModal at root level to handle first-time user profile setup globally
